content.title: Wolfie Studies Presentation Guide
lines: Purpose: Help student presenters explain research findings clearly to a mixed audience.
Audience: Faculty reviewers, invited students, and community guests.
Slide Guidance: Start with the problem, show the method, then end with takeaways and next steps.
Speaking Notes: Keep explanations plain-language, pause after each key chart, and define acronyms aloud.
Preparation: Rehearse the full talk twice, test embedded media, and prepare answers for likely questions.
Timing: Keep the main talk to eight minutes with two minutes for questions.

content.title: IDS 410: Advanced Data Stewardship Course Guide
lines: Course Overview: IDS 410 prepares students to manage research datasets from intake through publication, with emphasis on privacy, reproducibility, version control, and stakeholder communication.
Instructor: Dr. Lena Ortiz. Office Hours: Monday 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM and Wednesday 3:00 PM - 4:00 PM by appointment.
Learning Goals: Students will classify sensitive data, write data dictionaries, design retention plans, evaluate repository requirements, and explain stewardship decisions to nontechnical partners.
Required Materials: Research Data Management Handbook, institutional repository guide, Git basics worksheet, sample consent-language packet, and weekly case packets posted in the learning portal.
Weekly Rhythm: Monday sessions introduce methods, Wednesday labs apply those methods to messy datasets, and Friday check-ins resolve blockers before submission deadlines.
Major Assignments: Data inventory memo due September 18, privacy risk analysis due October 9, repository readiness plan due November 6, final stewardship portfolio due December 11.
Grading: Labs 20%, data inventory memo 15%, privacy risk analysis 20%, repository readiness plan 20%, final portfolio 20%, participation and peer review 5%.
Policy on Late Work: Students may use one 48-hour extension token before November 15; later requests require documentation and must be sent before the deadline.
Academic Integrity: All borrowed code, metadata templates, and AI-generated drafting support must be cited in the submission notes.
Communication: Use the course forum for general questions so answers are visible to the cohort; email the instructor only for private advising or accessibility issues.
Schedule Week 1: Orientation to data stewardship and review of campus repository policies.
Schedule Week 2: Dataset intake interviews, stakeholder maps, and first lab on ambiguous source files.
Schedule Week 3: Metadata standards, controlled vocabularies, and a data dictionary workshop.
Schedule Week 4: Privacy classification, consent constraints, and risk scoring for mixed public/private records.
Schedule Week 5: File naming, version control, and reproducibility checks using a shared project repository.
Schedule Week 6: Midterm review of data inventory memos and peer feedback on missing fields.
Schedule Week 7: Repository selection criteria, embargo rules, and license choices.
Schedule Week 8: Preservation formats, checksum validation, and retention schedule planning.
Schedule Week 9: Accessibility review for tables, documentation, and public-facing README files.
Schedule Week 10: Final portfolio clinic with emphasis on linking policy decisions to evidence.

content.title: Laboratory Access Policy Implementation Memo
lines: Laboratory Access Policy Implementation Memo
Purpose: This memo explains the updated process for granting after-hours laboratory access to research assistants, visiting students, and temporary project staff.
Background: The prior approval process relied on email threads and inconsistent spreadsheet notes, which made it difficult to confirm training status during safety audits.
Scope: The process applies to wet labs, secure computing rooms, shared equipment rooms, and storage spaces that require card access outside normal building hours.
Required Prerequisites: Supervisors must verify safety training, project assignment, emergency contact information, and expiration date before submitting a request.
Approval Workflow: Department coordinators review the request packet, facilities staff activate card permissions, and the research office performs a weekly exception review.
Documentation: Each request must include the student name, campus ID, sponsor, room list, access window, training certificate date, and justification for after-hours access.
Deadlines: Requests needed for Monday activation must be submitted by Thursday at 3:00 PM. Emergency requests require director approval and must be reconciled within two business days.
Audit Procedure: Coordinators must export the active-access report on the first business day of each month and compare it with project rosters and departure notices.
Revocation: Access must be removed within one business day when a project ends, a training certificate expires, or a supervisor reports a role change.
Follow-up: Send questions and exception requests to research-operations@example.edu with the subject line Lab Access Exception.
